books.title: Team of Rivals
books.author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
books.quote: His ambition was a little engine that knew no rest.
books.note: Lincoln didn't push his rivals aside — he pulled them into his cabinet. This book is a masterclass in emotional intelligence, political strategy, and what it means to lead people who once wanted your job. Reading it slowly, one chapter at a time, and every page feels like a lesson.

books.title: An Unfinished Life: John F. Kennedy, 1917–1963
books.author: Robert Dallek
books.quote: Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country.
books.note: The definitive JFK biography — the first to draw on his sealed medical records, revealing how a man in near-constant pain projected such effortless vigor. I want to read it for the gap between the myth and the human: Camelot on the surface, Addison's disease and back braces underneath. A study in how charisma and frailty can live in the same person.
